MOVING PARTY IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B AND PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT; OPPOSING PARTY IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B AND PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION. COURTESY COPIES TO BE DELIVERED NO LATER THAN JANUARY 2, 2018 WITH COVER LETTERS REFLECTING NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, NA, A CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURER TO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF CC 2924.12 IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND - CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATION OF RIGHTS REGARDING PAST WRONGS IS IMPROPER AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES HBOR VIOLATION RE: FAILURE TO CONTACT AND/OR FAILURE TO ATTEMPT TO CONTACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

Notice Of Motion And Wells Fargo'S Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Serop Hovsepian of JT Legal Group, APC, Telephone (818) 745-1558, appearing for Plaintiff Kim Mitchell;
Laszlo Ladi, Jr. of Severson & Werson, Telephone (415) 398-3344, appearing for Defendant Wells Fargo Bank;

The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modifications as follows:

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, NA,'S Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions.

Motion having been duly considered and no opposition thereto it is hereby ORDERED: there being good cause the Motion is GRANTED and pursuant to CCP 2023.030 (d) (2) the Plaintiff is stayed from any further proceedings until the discovery order of July 19, 2018 is obeyed. Additional monetary sanctions are awarded to the moving party Defendant Wells Fargo in the amount of $1,480.00 and Plaintiff is to pay those sanctions by October 1, 2018.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Dianne Peebles; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT).

Motion To Compel And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mohammad Maaz, JT Legal Group, APC, appearing for plaintiff Raymond Smith, Telephone 818-745-1556 (CourtCall appearance)
Laslo Ladi, Jr., Severson & Werson, appearing for defendnat, Wells Fargo Bank, Telphone 415-398-3344

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK'S Motion To Compel And For Monetary Sanctions.

Ruling: The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification.

NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES FROM PLAINTIFF. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AS FOLLOWS;

(1) PLAINTIFF WITHIN TEN (10) DAY FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER WILL PROVIDE WELLS FARGO WITH CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSE WITHOUT OBJECTION TO ITS REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION. IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SERVE SUCH RESPONSES WITHIN THE TIME SPECIFIED, ALL REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND PLAINTIFF WILL BE PRECLUDED TO INTRODUCE ANY EVEDINCE CHALLENGING THOSE ADMISSIONS.

(2) PLAINTIFF WITHIN TWENTY (20) FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER SERVE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION TO WELLS FARGO'S FORM INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE), SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET 0NE) AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET ONE)

(3) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,600.00, PAYMENT OF WHICH SHALL BE MADE TO DEFENDANT AND ITS COUNSEL WITHIN TEN (10) DAY FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER.

Order is signed in open court

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, NA, A CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurer to cause of action for violation of CC 2924.12 is sustained without leave to amend - cause of action for declaration of rights regarding past wrongs is improper and injunctive relief is not a cause of action. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges HBOR violation re: failure to contact and/or failure to attempt to contact. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
